market.title: ‘Quality publishers are stronger together’ – Ozone offers strength in numbers
market.summary_raw:
For many news publishers, the best chance of increasing their digital advertising revenues and market share is by being part of an advertising alliance. Launched in the UK in 2018 by four large UK publishers, Ozone has expanded to the United States and Europe, and today includes some of the world's biggest media brands.

The post ‘Quality publishers are stronger together’ – Ozone offers strength in numbers appeared first on WAN-IFRA.

market.title: Inspired tactics: A news subscription series – Part 2, Tech turns readers into subscribers
market.summary_raw:
In this second part of a four-part series on news subscriptions, guest writer Bihag Karnani looks at what occurs before the conversion; the moment when a reader decides if he/she will pay. "News media organisations treat the paywall as a barrier," he writes. "Technology companies treat the paywall as an evaluation mechanism. Do we request payment? At what point? How much?" This article looks at these questions and more.

The post Inspired tactics: A news subscription series – Part 2, Tech turns readers into subscribers appeared first on WAN-IFRA.

market.title: Mobilizer, connector, spectator, or outsider? New Pew research categorizes how Americans do (or don’t) engage civically
market.summary_raw: Civic engagement takes many forms. The term describes activities as disparate as voting, volunteering, attending religious services, and following the news. Yet “these behaviors are often studied separately, making it difficult to see how they come together in people’s day-to-day lives,” researchers from the Pew-Knight Initiative write in a new report published Thursday.
